message:
style: replace assert!(false, ...) with unreachable!() and expect_err()

Systematic cleanup of assertion patterns across the codebase. The
assert!(false, msg) + return pattern is verbose and misleading - it
looks like a conditional assertion but always fails. Replace with
idiomatic alternatives:

unreachable!() - for match arms that should never be reached:
- src/extensions.rs: 10 HostcallOutcome match arms in tests
- src/providers/mod.rs: 3 ContentBlock match arms
- src/tui.rs: 1 MarkdownChunk match arm
- tests/provider_factory.rs: 2 error variant match arms

expect_err() - for Result values expected to be errors:
- tests/provider_factory.rs: create_provider().expect_err() replaces
  match + panic!() blocks

assert!() - fix real bug:
- src/providers/mod.rs: change assert!(false, "expected cancelled.txt")
  to assert!(out_path.exists(), "expected cancelled.txt") - was always
  failing instead of checking file existence

Other idiom improvements:
- tests/common/tmux.rs: if !status { panic!() } -> assert!(status, ...)
- tests/e2e_cli.rs: same pattern
- tests/tools_conformance.rs: .map().unwrap_or() -> .is_ok_and()
- tests/tools_conformance.rs: .map().collect::<String>() -> for loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
